core_mechanism: linear (fixed-point) stability analysis of an autonomous dynamical system: classifying the equilibria of the governing flow equations by the local phase-portrait type of the linearized Jacobian (saddle points vs. centres) and testing their stability via a linearized time-dependent perturbation about the equilibria
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method as a 'dynamical systems analysis of the critical points' that classifies them as saddle and centre-type points and then uses a 'time-dependent perturbative study' to establish stability under perturbations, which is the standard equilibrium-classification-plus-linear-stability machinery.
